commit.message:
fix: macOS notifications now open console when clicked (#1649)

* Initial plan

* 🐛 fix: call event.preventDefault() in notification onclick to fix macOS opening Finder

Without event.preventDefault(), macOS handles the notification click
with its default OS behavior, which can launch Finder or the app icon
location instead of focusing the browser window and navigating to the
console deep link URL.

Fixed in both sendNotificationWithDeepLink (useDeepLink.ts) and the
inline notification handler in MiniDashboard.tsx.

fix(packaging): include hwdb and quirks in deb/rpm/tarball packages (#82)

Linux deb, rpm, and tarball packages were missing the hwdb file
(joystick classification) and modprobe quirks file (ALWAYS_POLL for
Asetek/Simagic). Without these, installed packages would not
provide full plug-and-play support.

Changes:
- Tarball: include hwdb and quirks files alongside udev rules
- Deb: install to /etc/udev/hwdb.d/ and /etc/modprobe.d/
- RPM: install to same paths with config(noreplace) protection
- Both deb and rpm postinst scripts now run systemd-hwdb update
- Tarball README updated with complete manual install steps

commit.message:
feat(packaging): add Linux hwdb entries for joystick classification (#79)

Add 99-racing-wheel-suite.hwdb with ID_INPUT_JOYSTICK=1 entries for all
supported racing peripherals. Without these entries, Linux may
misclassify devices (especially pedals) as accelerometers instead of
joysticks, breaking SDL/Proton/Steam game detection.

Covers 90+ devices across 20+ manufacturers:
- All wheelbases (FFB devices)
- All pedals, shifters, handbrakes, and button boxes

Also updates install.sh to install the hwdb file and run
systemd-hwdb update, and adds packaging validation tests.

Source: JacKeTUs/simracing-hwdb format specification

* Update styles.css

* Fix Android shell: channel completed prematurely by stderr reader

Both stdout and stderr readers independently called TryComplete() on the
shared channel. If stderr closed early (no error output), it completed
the channel, preventing any further stdout data from being delivered.

Fix: track active reader count and only complete the channel when ALL
readers are done using Interlocked.Decrement.

commit.message:
[#10168] Fail fast when file: URI source is missing in JobManager.fetchFileFromUri (#10212)

### What changes were proposed in this pull request?

Validate that the source path exists before creating a symlink in
`fetchFileFromUri`. Previously, a dangling symlink was silently created
when the source file did not exist, causing failures downstream instead
of at fetch time.

### Why are the changes needed?

`JobManager.fetchFileFromUri` currently reports success for `file:` URIs
by creating a symlink in the staging directory, even when the source
path does not exist. This creates a dangling symlink and returns a
seemingly valid staged path, causing failures later when the file is
consumed instead of failing at fetch time.

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

No. This is an internal defensive check — the external API is unchanged.
Jobs that previously failed late with a confusing error will now fail
early with a clear message.

### How was this patch tested?

Added unit test `testFetchFileFromUriWithMissingLocalFileShouldFail` (as
suggested in #10168) that:
1. Creates a staging directory
2. Constructs a `file:` URI pointing to a non-existent path
3. Asserts that `fetchFileFromUri` throws `RuntimeException`

Closes #10168

commit.message:
fix: cache generated OG images in-process with IMemoryCache

ImageSharp PNG encoding was running on every /og/events/{id}/image request
with no server-side cache. On a cold EKS pod (t3.small) this exceeded the
30s test timeout. The HTTP Cache-Control headers were set but no
UseResponseCaching middleware was registered, so ASP.NET was regenerating
the image on every hit.

Fix: inject IMemoryCache into OgController and cache rendered PNG bytes
keyed by event ID (TTL 24h, SizeLimit 500 entries). The gradient is
deterministic from the event ID so caching is always correct.

Also revert the band-aid test.setTimeout(60s) added in the previous commit.

fix(packaging): add Simagic EVO ALWAYS_POLL quirk for Linux (#80)

Simagic GT Neo / EVO wheelbases require HID_QUIRK_ALWAYS_POLL (0x0400)
to prevent the 'infinite reboot' disconnect loop on Linux. Without
continuous polling, the device repeatedly disconnects and reconnects.

Adds EVO Sport (0x3670:0x0500), EVO (0x3670:0x0501), and EVO Pro
(0x3670:0x0502) to the quirks configuration.

Source: JacKeTUs/linux-steering-wheels 'Device is infinitely rebooting'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: v1.3.0 — Phases 1-5 complete (bugfixes, security, UX, new features)

Phase 1 — Critical Bugfixes:
- Thread safety: threading.Lock on all shared ClipboardMonitor state
- SQLite WAL mode, busy_timeout=3000, integrity_check+auto-backup on corrupt DB
- Win32 OpenClipboard retry (3x, 50ms), CF_EXCLUDECLIPBOARDCONTENT detection
- WM_WTSSESSION_CHANGE handler to re-sync after Windows lock/unlock
- Hotkey conflict detection: tray notification when RegisterHotKey fails
- OCR moved to QThread worker with 10s timeout; disable button if Tesseract missing
- Duplicate suppression: _ignore_next_change flag before every clipboard write
- Image orphan cleanup on startup; atomic image deletion with DB record

Phase 2 — Security:
- sensitive_detector.py: credit card regex + password/token label masking
- Masked text stored only, never plain; lock icon in item widget
- Ghost Mode tray icon change (gray 'G' when active)
- Crash logging: RotatingFileHandler to %APPDATA%/CyberClip/logs/crash.log

Phase 3 — Performance:
- 300ms debounce on search input
- History size limit (MAX_HISTORY_SIZE=500) enforced on insert
- Image viewer zoom clamped to ZOOM_MIN(10%)–ZOOM_MAX(500%)

Phase 4 — UX:
- Tray tooltip shows queue position [N/M]
- Paste All confirmation dialog when queue > 10 items
- Dynamic tray menu rebuilt on each right-click; shows last 5 items
- Drag-drop order persisted to 'position' column in SQLite

Phase 5 — New Features:
- quick_paste_popup.py: Ctrl+Shift+Space frameless popup at cursor; 1-9 to paste
- text_transforms.py: 14 transforms (case, trim, URL/Base64/JSON, dedup lines)
- Smart type detection: URL, email, code, color, file path tags + icons
- Snippets: save any clip with name+trigger keyword, never auto-deleted
- Export/Import: JSON with base64 images, skips exact duplicates on import
- Per-app exclusion list: block process names from clipboard recording

Phase 6 — Code Quality:
- ServiceLocator pattern in app.py, DI constructor injection
- Pinned requirements: PyQt6==6.7.0, pywin32==306, Pillow==10.3.0, pytesseract==0.3.10, psutil==5.9.8

commit.message:
Fix MCP server loading — pass full config including tools and type

Root cause: loadMcpFromProject stripped tools and type fields from
.vscode/mcp.json configs. The SDK requires tools: string[] to register
which tools to expose from each server. Without it, servers were silently
skipped.

Fixes:
- Pass through all fields from mcp.json (type, tools, timeout, etc.)
- Default tools to [*] when not specified
- Default args to [] (required by SDK, was undefined)
- Updated McpServerConfig interface with tools, type, timeout fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Signed-off-by: Adriano Meligrana <68152031+Tortar@users.noreply.github.com>

* dix

Signed-off-by: Adriano Meligrana <68152031+Tortar@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix random function to use element-wise multiplication

Signed-off-by: Adriano Meligrana <68152031+Tortar@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add zero_derivative rules for XoshiroSimd bulk operations

xoshiro_bulk_simd and xoshiro_bulk_nosimd use llvmcall intrinsics
which Mooncake cannot differentiate. This caused the derived rule
test for `x -> x .* rand(Xoshiro(123), size(x)...)` to fail, since
which dispatches through a Sampler to xoshiro_bulk_simd.

Adding @zero_derivative rules for these functions lets Mooncake run
the primal computation (filling the array with random values) while
returning zero gradients for all inputs, analogous to the existing
rule for MersenneTwister which avoids its ccall.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Bump version to 0.5.14

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve clarity of random rules

- Unify ccall/llvmcall avoidance comment into one block
- Condense xoshiro_bulk signatures to single lines with parameter
  annotations (dst, len, eltype, [simd_width,] bits2float)
- Rename testset 'randn' -> 'random' (now covers all random functions)
- Remove inconsistent Random. qualification in state advancement test
